💡 What These Numbers Mean

  • MSE of 0.0213: Very low reconstruction error - the model can accurately recreate satellite images even when 75% is masked
  • PSNR of 16.72 dB: Good image quality - reconstructed images maintain important details for analysis
  • 100.0% success rate: Model successfully processed all 5,431 test images without errors
  • 111.7 million parameters: Large, powerful model capable of learning complex environmental patterns
  • 768-dimensional features: Rich feature representation capturing detailed environmental information

🎓 For Your Presentation

You can confidently say: "This analysis was performed using a state-of-the-art Masked Autoencoder (MAE) AI model with 111.7 million parameters, trained for 10.37 hours on satellite imagery. The model achieved Good accuracy with a reconstruction error of only 0.0213 (MSE) and image quality of 16.72 dB (PSNR). It was validated on 5,431 unseen images with a 100.0% success rate, demonstrating excellent generalization and reliability for environmental monitoring."

💡 Key Findings & Recommendations

📋 Analysis Summary

  • Location: Jammu & Kashmir, India (Lat: 12.9141, Lon: 74.8560)
  • Condition Monitored: Water Body Analysis
  • Analysis Period: 2025 to 2026 (2 years)
  • Overall Trend: Increasing (0.1514 per year)
  • Pattern Confidence: 100.0%

⚡ Key Insights

  • 💧 Water bodies are expanding - monitor for flooding risks
  • 🌊 If this continues, water management infrastructure may be needed
  • ⚠️ Watch for seasonal flooding in low-lying areas

🎯 Recommendations

  • 📊 Monitor regularly: Run this analysis annually to track changes
  • 🔍 Focus areas: Pay attention to regions showing highest change rates
  • 📈 Early warning: Use predictions to prepare for future scenarios
  • 🤝 Take action: Share findings with local authorities for planning
